name: Climb to the ports of Tabladillo and Casasana — circular via the Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud
name_original: Subida a los puertos de Tabladillo y Casasana  — circular por el Monasterio de Santa María de Monsalud
region_intro: Road cycling around Sacedón is characterized by its diverse landscapes, featuring expansive reservoirs and rolling terrain. The region offers a mix of paved roads with varying elevations, providing both challenging climbs and enjoyable descents. Cyclists can experience routes that traverse areas near the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Embalse de Buendía, as well as roads leading towards the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The terrain includes both gentle sections and more demanding climbs, catering to different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Sacedón The most popular road cycling route is Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop, a 65.5 miles (105.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers continuous views of the Buendía reservoir and passes near the ancient Roman city of Ercávica. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pastrana and Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop, a difficult 46.6 miles (74.9 km) path. This route provides scenic vistas of the Entrepeñas reservoir and includes sections near the historic town of Pastrana. Local road cyclists also love the Climb to the ports of Tabladillo and Casasana — circular via the Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud, a 27.2 miles (43.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with climbs, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Road cycling around Sacedón is defined by its large reservoirs, rolling terrain, and varied elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding routes with significant climbs. The routes in Sacedón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.0 from 1 review. More than 150 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sacedón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Sacedón?
A: Road cycling around Sacedón features diverse landscapes, including expansive reservoirs and rolling terrain. You'll find a mix of mostly paved roads with varying elevations, offering both challenging climbs and enjoyable descents. Routes often traverse areas near the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Embalse de Buendía, as well as roads leading towards the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The region provides a blend of gentle sections and more demanding climbs, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sacedón?
A: Yes, Sacedón offers routes for various skill levels. While many routes feature significant climbs, there are options with more manageable ascents. For example, the region includes routes with elevation gains under 500 feet for easier rides. Out of the 14 available routes, one is rated as easy and three as moderate, providing good starting points for less experienced cyclists.

Q: What are the best times of year to go road cycling in Sacedón?
A: The best times to visit Sacedón for outdoor activities, including road cycling, are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather conditions are milder and more pleasant, making for a more enjoyable experience on the roads.

Q: What natural features will I see along the road cycling routes in Sacedón?
A: Sacedón is uniquely positioned amidst the vast waters of the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Embalse de Buendía, which create stunning vistas and provide picturesque backdrops for many routes. You'll also encounter varied terrain, from areas near these reservoirs to roads leading towards the rugged Sierra de Caldereros and the extensive natural landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while road cycling in Sacedón?
A: Yes, you can incorporate cultural stops into your rides. Notable historical sites include the ancient Roman city of Ercávica, which is near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014635660/buendia-reservoir-and-ercavica-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3764775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt; are also significant man-made structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018716240/climb-to-the-ports-of-tabladillo-and-casasana-circular-via-the-monastery-of-santa-maria-de-monsalud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the ports of Tabladillo and Casasana — circular via the Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud&lt;/a&gt; passes near the historic Monsalud Monastery, showcasing Cistercian architecture.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Sacedón?
A: The road cycling routes around Sacedón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 1.0 from 1 review. Cyclists often praise the continuous views of the large reservoirs, the varied terrain that offers both challenging climbs and enjoyable descents, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Sacedón?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Sacedón are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014635660/buendia-reservoir-and-ercavica-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014637928/pastrana-and-entrepenas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pastrana and Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018716240/climb-to-the-ports-of-tabladillo-and-casasana-circular-via-the-monastery-of-santa-maria-de-monsalud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the ports of Tabladillo and Casasana — circular via the Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some of the most scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers numerous scenic viewpoints, particularly overlooking the vast reservoirs. You can find stunning vistas at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Dam Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Cycling around the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Embalse de Buendía provides continuous water views.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Sacedón?
A: Yes, Sacedón offers several longer routes for those seeking a more extensive ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014635660/buendia-reservoir-and-ercavica-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Reservoir and Ercávica Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 65.5-mile (105.4 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Estación de Auñón&lt;/a&gt; is an even longer 124.5 km route.

Q: What other outdoor activities can I do in Sacedón besides road cycling?
A: Beyond road cycling, Sacedón and its surroundings offer a wealth of other outdoor activities. You can enjoy hiking on numerous trails, water sports like fishing and kayaking on the reservoirs, and even via ferratas for adventure seekers. The nearby Alto Tajo Natural Park also provides opportunities for exploring extensive natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, for those interested in natural water features, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Estación de Auñón&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that includes the Cifuentes River Waterfall as a point of interest.
tips: The Pareja dam, dam or lake, is an infrastructure belonging to the Entrepeñas reservoir, located next to the town of Pareja, taking advantage of the waters of the Ompolveda River.
Nice climb without a step of 5.5 km from Pareja to Tabladillo.
Puerto de Casasana with magnificent views of Pareja and surroundings.

Just 5 minutes from the town of Córcoles are the ruins of the monastery that was part of the Cistercian complex dating from the second half of the 12th century and is a clear example of the presence of this Order throughout the Iberian Peninsula. The ruins can be visited, check the official website:
https://cultura.castillalamancha.es/patrimonio/yacimientos-visitables/monasterio-de-monsalud

waypoints.name: Monastery of Santa María de Monsalud
